methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Nyobolt is a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Sai Shivareddy and Clare Grey. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company (LNZA) operating in the same sector and stage. The private peer set includes Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc., among others.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $100M+ total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B progression, implying a $190M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the 6-company same-stage, same-sector comparable set yields a median valuation of $355M, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in company maturity and technology risk within climate tech.

Synthesizing both methods produces a valuation range of $162M (low), $272M (median), and $408M (high). The stage-progression method anchors toward the lower end given reliance on historical raise size, while peer-median pulls meaningfully higher. Key uncertainties include Nyobolt's position relative to the peer range on technology de-risking, capital efficiency, and path to commercialization. The peer set's wide dispersion—driven partly by Commonwealth Fusion Systems' $5B outlier valuation—suggests meaningful dispersion around any central estimate remains appropriate.
